results.title: 423 Years of American History in 13 Minutes
results.reason: High reach and entertaining pace make it an excellent entry point for beginners, though it sacrifices depth for speed.
summary: How the first colonies started in America
The fight for freedom from British rule
Major wars that changed the nation's shape
Key events leading up to modern times

results.title: The history of our world in 18 minutes | David Christian | TED
results.reason: A highly popular TED talk that explains the 'why' behind historical complexity, though it leans toward a 'Big History' perspective.
summary: How the entire universe began long ago
The way life grew on our planet
How humans built complex modern societies
The big picture of our shared story

results.title: Timeline of World History
results.reason: The visual chart format provides exceptional structure for beginners to understand the chronological flow of modern history.
summary: When the first great civilizations appeared
Which powerful empires rose and then fell
How different cultures connected over time
A visual map of human history's path

results.title: The Mess That Is Early Modern Europe (1500 - 1700s) Explained: A Complete Overview
results.reason: Offers the best depth for a 'complete overview,' though the long duration may be intimidating for some beginners.
summary: The religious changes that split Europe apart
How kings and queens gained total power
The start of global trade and travel
New ways of thinking about the world

results.title: Early Modern Europe Explained in 11 minutes
results.reason: Specifically targets the early modern period with a clear, digestible narrative suited for those new to the era.
summary: The end of the old Middle Ages
How art and science began to grow
The impact of the first printing press
Why European borders changed so much
